title: Apple’s new Studio displays come with Thunderbolt 5
summary:
Apple has unveiled its latest lineup of products, including the new MacBook Pro and MacBook Air, both equipped with advanced chips, alongside a new family of displays. The highlight of this launch is the introduction of the Studio Display and the Studio Display XDR, which are designed to enhance user experience with superior image quality and connectivity options. The Studio Display, priced at $1,599, features a 5K Retina display with over 14 million pixels, a 12MP Center Stage camera, and support for Desk View, allowing users to showcase both their face and workspace simultaneously. The display also boasts Thunderbolt 5 ports, enabling daisy-chaining of up to four displays, and includes a three-microphone array and a six-speaker sound system that delivers 30% deeper bass compared to its predecessor.

The Studio Display XDR, priced at $3,299, takes performance a step further with its 5K Retina XDR display, which offers a resolution of 5120×2880 and features a mini-LED backlight with over 2,000 local dimming zones. This model can achieve brightness levels of up to 1000 nits for standard dynamic range (SDR) and 2000 nits for peak high dynamic range (HDR), along with an impressive contrast ratio of 1,000,000:1. Additionally, it supports the Adobe RGB color standard, making it suitable for professional use in various fields, including medical imaging, thanks to its DICOM presets and a Medical Imaging Calibrator that is pending FDA clearance.

With these new displays, Apple aims to cater to a wide range of users, from creative professionals to those in specialized fields like healthcare. The integration of advanced features such as Adaptive Sync and a high refresh rate of 120Hz further enhances the usability of these displays, making them ideal for both work and entertainment. As Apple continues to innovate in the tech space, these products reflect its commitment to providing high-quality, versatile solutions for modern users.
social_text: Apple's new Studio Display ($1,599) and Studio Display XDR ($3,299) feature 5K Retina displays, Thunderbolt 5 connectivity, and advanced audio systems, enhancing productivity and user experience significantly.

title: Apple unveils M5 Pro and M5 Max chips with new ‘Fusion Architecture’
summary:
Apple has officially launched its latest M-series chips, the M5 Pro and M5 Max, designed to enhance the performance of the new MacBook Pro lineup. These chips are built on Apple's innovative Fusion Architecture, which integrates two dies into a single, high-performance system on a chip (SoC). This architecture includes a powerful CPU, a scalable GPU, a Media Engine, a unified memory controller, a Neural Engine, and Thunderbolt 5 capabilities, marking a significant advancement in Apple's chip technology.

The M5 Pro and M5 Max chips feature an 18-core CPU, an upgrade from the previous M4 Pro and M4 Max configurations. The new CPU design includes six “super cores” and twelve performance cores, resulting in a performance boost of up to 30% for professional workloads. Additionally, the GPU has been enhanced to support up to 40 cores, delivering over four times the peak GPU compute for AI tasks compared to the previous generation, with overall graphics performance improving by 20% and ray-tracing workloads seeing a 35% enhancement.

Memory capabilities have also been upgraded, with the M5 Pro supporting up to 64GB of unified memory and a bandwidth of 307GB/s, while the M5 Max continues to support up to 128GB with an increased bandwidth of 614GB/s. These advancements position Apple’s new chips as formidable competitors in the tech market, catering to professionals who demand high performance and efficiency in their computing tasks.
social_text: Apple's M5 Pro and M5 Max chips debut with Fusion Architecture, featuring an 18-core CPU and up to 40-core GPU. Performance boosts include 30% for pro workloads and 20% for graphics, with M5 Max supporting 128GB memory at 614GB/s bandwidth.

title: Fig Security emerges from stealth with $38M to help security teams deal with change
summary:
Fig Security has emerged from stealth mode with a significant $38 million in seed and Series A funding to enhance the capabilities of security teams in managing complex enterprise tech stacks. Founded by veterans from Israel's elite cyber and data intelligence units, the startup aims to address the challenges faced by security teams in monitoring and maintaining the integrity of their security tools. The modern security landscape is fraught with intricacies, where a minor change in one tool can lead to unpredictable consequences, potentially compromising an organization's detection and response capabilities.

The core technology developed by Fig Security focuses on tracing data flows within the security stack, from the initial data sources through various pipelines to the final security orchestration and automation platforms. This comprehensive monitoring allows security teams to receive alerts when changes in the system impact their detection or response capabilities. Additionally, Fig's platform enables organizations to simulate the effects of new fixes or changes before they are implemented, thereby minimizing risks associated with updates.

CEO and co-founder Gal Shafir emphasizes the importance of detection as the 'single source of truth' in security operations. By back-tracing the health of detection mechanisms, Fig Security provides actionable insights that help organizations ensure their security measures remain effective amidst constant changes. This innovative approach not only enhances the reliability of security systems but also empowers teams to proactively manage their security posture in an increasingly complex digital environment.
social_text: Fig Security has raised $38M to help security teams monitor their tech stacks, ensuring detection and response capabilities remain intact amidst changes. Their platform simulates impacts of updates before deployment.

title: Apple bakes in AI smarts into its new $599 iPhone 17e
summary:
Apple has officially launched the iPhone 17e, a budget-friendly smartphone priced at $599, set to hit the market on March 11. This new model is powered by the A19 chip, which is also found in the base iPhone 17, and it comes with a significant upgrade in storage, offering 256 GB as standard—double the entry-level storage of its predecessor. The iPhone 17e introduces MagSafe and Qi2 technology, allowing for wireless charging capabilities of up to 15W, enhancing user convenience and charging efficiency. Available in black, white, and a new soft pink color, the iPhone 17e aims to appeal to a broad audience looking for affordability without sacrificing performance or features.

In terms of connectivity, the iPhone 17e is equipped with Apple's latest C1X cellular modem, which boasts speeds up to twice as fast as the previous C1 modem used in the iPhone 16e, while also being 30% more energy-efficient. This improvement in connectivity is crucial for users who rely heavily on mobile data for streaming and browsing. The camera system remains impressive, featuring a 48-megapixel camera that includes a Fusion camera with optical-quality 2x Telephoto capabilities. This allows users to capture high-quality images and videos, making the iPhone 17e a strong contender in the budget smartphone market.

Video recording capabilities have also been enhanced, with the ability to shoot in 4K with Dolby Vision at 60 fps, providing a cinematic experience. Additionally, the iPhone 17e supports Spatial Audio recording, which offers immersive sound experiences when paired with AirPods or Apple Vision Pro. With these advancements, Apple continues to solidify its position in the competitive smartphone landscape, catering to consumers who seek advanced technology at a more accessible price point.
social_text: Apple's new iPhone 17e, priced at $599, features the A19 chip, 256 GB storage, MagSafe, and Qi2 for 15W wireless charging. It includes a 48MP camera and C1X modem for double the speed and 30% energy savings.

title: Stock market today: Dow plunges over 1,000 points, S&P 500 and Nasdaq sink as oil surges amid war worries
summary:
On Tuesday, U.S. stock markets experienced a significant sell-off, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeting over 1,100 points, marking a decline of more than 2%. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite also fell sharply, each dropping over 2%. This downturn was largely attributed to escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly following new airstrikes by Israel and the U.S. on Iran. The renewed conflict has raised fears of a prolonged regional war, which has led to increased volatility in the markets and heightened concerns about oil supply disruptions. As a result, crude oil prices surged by over 8.5%, further exacerbating inflation worries among investors.

The geopolitical situation has created a ripple effect across various sectors, with Wall Street bracing for potential impacts on global markets. President Trump's comments regarding the possibility of U.S. military involvement have intensified these concerns, as he indicated that the U.S. could remain engaged for an extended period. The market's reaction reflects a broader anxiety about how these developments could affect economic stability and corporate earnings in the near future.

In addition to geopolitical factors, investors are closely monitoring corporate earnings reports. Retail giant Target's shares rose in premarket trading after it reported holiday sales that met Wall Street expectations, despite being considered lackluster. Other companies, including Ross Stores, AutoZone, and Best Buy, are also set to release their earnings, which could further influence market sentiment as investors weigh the implications of both geopolitical tensions and corporate performance on the economy.
social_text: U.S. stocks fell sharply as the Dow dropped over 1,100 points amid escalating Middle East tensions. Oil prices surged 8.5% due to supply concerns, while investors eye corporate earnings from Target and others.

title: 908 Devices (MASS) Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript
summary:
908 Devices has reported impressive financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, showcasing a robust growth trajectory driven by its strategic focus on health, safety, and defense technology. The company achieved $56.2 million in revenue from continuing operations, marking an 18% year-over-year increase. This growth is attributed to the successful adoption of their innovative Explorer device, which can detect and quantify over 5,000 gas and vapor chemical threats in seconds. The transition from outdated FTIR equipment to modern devices has been a significant catalyst for this growth, with over 50% of device placements in 2025 stemming from this shift.

In addition to revenue growth, 908 Devices reported a remarkable 22% increase in recurring revenue, which now constitutes 35% of total revenues for the year. This surge is largely driven by the high demand for the Explorer device among first responders, as well as the introduction of the Viper product, which simplifies chemical analysis for solids and liquids. The company's focus on enhancing customer value through service and support has strengthened revenue visibility and long-term predictability, further solidifying its market position.

The fourth quarter also saw the company achieve a positive adjusted EBITDA of $700,000, a significant turnaround from a $4 million loss in the previous year. This improvement underscores the effectiveness of the structural initiatives implemented during their transformation. Overall, 908 Devices is well-positioned for continued growth as it addresses critical public health and safety challenges with its advanced technology solutions.
social_text: 908 Devices achieved $56.2M in revenue for 2025, an 18% YoY growth, driven by the Explorer device and a 22% rise in recurring revenue. Positive adjusted EBITDA of $700K marks a turnaround from a $4M loss last year.

title: ‘Can’t they read in Washington?’: Jim Rogers dumps US stocks due to ballooning national debt. Protect your nest egg now
summary:
Jim Rogers, a prominent investor, has recently divested from U.S. stocks, citing the alarming rise in the national debt, which has reached $38.56 trillion as of February 2026. This staggering figure positions the U.S. as the largest debtor nation in history, raising significant concerns about the sustainability of the country's financial policies. Rogers expresses skepticism about the ability of policymakers to manage the economic fallout, suggesting that even the Federal Reserve may not have the resources to mitigate the impending crisis. His comments reflect a broader sentiment among investors who are increasingly wary of the volatility in the markets, particularly in light of recent fluctuations in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq indexes, which have been influenced by weak technology returns and inflationary pressures.

In the current economic climate, characterized by uncertainty and potential downturns, Rogers advocates for a defensive investment strategy. He emphasizes the importance of careful positioning to protect one's financial interests during these turbulent times. Despite the challenges, he notes that there may still be opportunities for profit before a significant market decline occurs. His shift towards investing in emerging markets, such as Uzbekistan, indicates a strategic pivot away from traditional U.S. equities, which he believes are becoming increasingly risky.

Rogers' warnings serve as a wake-up call for investors to reassess their portfolios and consider the implications of rising national debt on market stability. With nearly 50% of Americans reportedly struggling financially, as highlighted by financial expert Dave Ramsey, the urgency for prudent financial management has never been greater. As the economic landscape continues to evolve, investors must remain vigilant and informed to safeguard their assets against potential downturns.
social_text: Jim Rogers exits U.S. stocks due to the national debt hitting $38.56 trillion, the highest in history. He warns of market volatility and suggests investing in emerging markets like Uzbekistan for better opportunities.

title: Oil prices surge as analysts say Iran conflict 'may be different' from previous flare-ups
summary:
The recent escalation of conflict involving Iran has led to a significant surge in oil prices, with analysts suggesting that this situation may differ from previous conflicts. Since the commencement of airstrikes by the US and Israel, which resulted in the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, crude oil prices have risen approximately 15%. This spike is notable as it comes amid a backdrop of heightened tensions in the Middle East, with futures for Brent crude and West Texas Intermediate experiencing a more than 9% increase. The market's response has been swift, but uncertainty looms over whether these price increases will be sustained or if they will revert to previous levels as seen in past conflicts.

Analysts have pointed out that the current situation has already led to tangible disruptions in shipping and insurance markets, which could further complicate the oil supply chain. Unlike previous flare-ups, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps has taken a more aggressive stance by closing the Strait of Hormuz, a critical passage for global oil shipments. This closure, coupled with reports of attacks on oil tankers, has created a heightened sense of geopolitical risk that is influencing market dynamics. The psychological impact of these developments is significant, as traders reassess their positions in light of potential long-term disruptions.

The ramifications of this conflict extend beyond immediate price fluctuations, as energy-linked facilities in the Gulf are increasingly becoming targets. Saudi Arabia's response to these developments will be crucial in shaping the future of oil prices and market stability. As the situation evolves, stakeholders in the energy sector are urged to remain vigilant and prepared for further volatility, as the implications of this conflict could have lasting effects on global oil supply and pricing strategies.
social_text: Oil prices surged 15% following US and Israel airstrikes on Iran, killing Ayatollah Khamenei. The Revolutionary Guard closed the Strait of Hormuz, raising geopolitical risks and impacting shipping routes significantly.

title: Everything you need to know about the new IRS Schedule 1-A tax breaks
summary:
The IRS has introduced Schedule 1-A (Form 1040) for the 2025 tax filing season, which allows taxpayers to claim new deductions stemming from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA). This form is designed to help individuals determine their eligibility for specific tax breaks, including exemptions on qualified cash tip income, overtime income, and vehicle loan interest. Additionally, seniors aged 65 and older can benefit from an increased deduction, making this a significant change for many taxpayers. Schedule 1-A is applicable to both those who take the standard deduction and those who itemize their deductions, thereby broadening its reach.

The OBBBA, enacted on July 4, 2025, aims to provide financial relief to various sectors, particularly those reliant on tips, such as restaurant and hospitality workers. The IRS has outlined a comprehensive list of qualifying occupations, ensuring that eligible taxpayers can take full advantage of these new deductions. The
social_text: Schedule 1-A (Form 1040) introduces new tax breaks for 2025, including no tax on qualified cash tips, overtime income, and vehicle loan interest, benefiting service workers and seniors with increased deductions.

title: Gaia (GAIA) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary:
In the Q4 2025 earnings call for Gaia, Inc., the company highlighted significant growth and strategic shifts within its business model. Approximately two-thirds of direct members have remained loyal for over a year, indicating a strong community foundation that enhances long-term value. The company emphasized its commitment to investing in artificial intelligence (AI) and community engagement, which has resulted in double retention rates and nearly double the revenue per member compared to third-party platforms. This success has led Gaia to focus its resources on its direct platform, as it offers unique capabilities that align with the company's future vision.

A leadership transition was also announced during the call, with Jonathan Nyuta stepping in as the new Chief Operating Officer following the conclusion of James Calhoun's contract. Nyuta's extensive experience, including previous roles at Gaia and other companies, is expected to bolster operational and product leadership as the company aims to enhance execution and build momentum. This change comes at a crucial time as Gaia seeks to solidify its position in the market and leverage its strengths in AI and community-driven initiatives.

The company's financial performance in Q4 was robust, with revenue reaching $25.5 million and an annualized run rate of approximately $100 million. Subscriber growth remained strong, with an addition of 20,000 members in the quarter. Furthermore, Gaia generated around $5 million in free cash flow, showcasing improved operating efficiency. These results reflect Gaia's strategic focus on its direct channel and its commitment to fostering a loyal subscriber base while advancing its AI capabilities.
social_text: Gaia, Inc. reported Q4 2025 revenue of $25.5M, with a $100M annualized run rate. Subscriber growth added 20,000 members, while free cash flow reached $5M, highlighting strong operational efficiency and community loyalty.

title: Goldman Sachs turns bullish on this coffee chain after recent pullback, calls growth rate ‘best in class’
summary:
Goldman Sachs has upgraded Dutch Bros, a popular coffee chain, from a neutral to a buy rating, citing its strong competitive position and impressive same-store sales growth. The investment bank maintains a 12-month price target of $75, indicating a potential upside of 40% from the current stock price. Despite a challenging year where shares have dropped 12% and nearly 26% in the last six months, analysts believe the recent pullback presents a prime opportunity for investors. They highlight Dutch Bros' robust growth story, which is characterized by solid same-store sales growth driven primarily by transaction growth and strong unit economics.

Analyst Christine Cho emphasized that Dutch Bros has a unique competitive moat that is difficult for rivals to replicate. The company is not only a leader in the coffee market but also excels in customized energy drinks, appealing particularly to younger consumers. Cho believes that the market has underestimated Dutch Bros' fundamental strengths, which position it well against established competitors. The company’s innovative strategies, including mobile ordering and an expanded food menu, are expected to enhance customer frequency and engagement, especially during peak morning hours.

Furthermore, Cho noted that Dutch Bros is adopting a more balanced development strategy, with new stores demonstrating higher productivity. The introduction of walk-up window stores in Los Angeles is seen as a promising step that could expand the company's total addressable market significantly. With a strong foundation and growth potential, Dutch Bros is poised for continued success in the competitive coffee landscape, making it an attractive investment opportunity according to Goldman Sachs.
social_text: Goldman Sachs upgraded Dutch Bros to buy, citing its strong competitive moat and same-store sales growth. With a $75 price target, there's a potential 40% upside despite a 12% drop this year. Analysts see growth driven by mobile orders and food offerings.

title: Steve Eisman says investors should ignore U.S.-Iran war, will be long-term 'positive'
summary:
Steve Eisman, renowned for his role in 'The Big Short', recently shared his perspective on the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict during an appearance on CNBC's 'Squawk Box'. Despite the immediate turmoil in the stock market following the U.S. and Israel's joint military action that resulted in the death of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Eisman believes that investors should not alter their strategies in response to the conflict. He argues that while oil prices may spike in the short term, the long-term outlook remains positive for the markets. Eisman emphasized that historical data shows geopolitical events typically have minimal lasting impact on stock performance, with the S&P 500 often returning to pre-event levels within a month.

Eisman pointed out that the current market conditions were already precarious, with stocks nearing record highs and concerns about the implications of artificial intelligence on the economy. He acknowledged that while the immediate effects of the conflict could pressure the stock market, the historical trend suggests that investors should remain calm and not make hasty decisions based on short-term volatility. He expressed support for President Trump's actions against what he described as a 'death cult', indicating a belief in the necessity of the military response.

In conclusion, Eisman's insights reflect a broader sentiment among some investors who view geopolitical conflicts as temporary disruptions rather than long-term threats. He cautioned that while the situation may evolve and take longer than anticipated, the fundamentals of the market remain strong, and investors should focus on the bigger picture rather than react impulsively to current events. This perspective encourages a measured approach to investing during times of geopolitical uncertainty.
social_text: Steve Eisman advises investors to stay the course amid the U.S.-Iran conflict, citing historical data showing minimal lasting impact on stocks. He believes the long-term outlook remains positive despite short-term oil price spikes.

title: Here are Monday's biggest analyst calls: Nvidia, Apple, Netflix, Amazon, CrowdStrike, Blue Owl, Twilio & more
summary:
On Monday, Wall Street analysts made significant calls regarding several major companies, reflecting a mix of upgrades and downgrades based on market conditions and company performance. Morgan Stanley highlighted Nvidia as a top pick, suggesting that despite the stock's stagnation over the past two quarters, the company's fundamentals remain strong. They anticipate a shift in sentiment towards Nvidia as enthusiasm for its growth prospects in 2027 builds, recommending an entry point at 18 times the company's projected earnings per share for that year.

In contrast, Barclays downgraded Blue Owl from overweight to equal weight, citing overly optimistic earnings estimates. This decision reflects a cautious approach as analysts believe the stock is now fairly valued based on its growth potential. Meanwhile, Piper Sandler upgraded CrowdStrike to overweight, encouraging investors to buy the dip after a 21% decline in shares year-to-date. They maintain a price target of $520, arguing that the market's bearish sentiment surrounding AI's impact on security is overblown for a company with a strong track record.

Additionally, Stifel upgraded Restaurant Brands International to buy, projecting robust growth for the parent company of Burger King, while Goldman Sachs initiated coverage of Forgent Power Solutions with a buy rating. JPMorgan also upgraded TotalEnergies and Eni, indicating that both energy stocks are well-positioned amidst potential disruptions in the Middle East. These analyst calls reflect a dynamic market landscape, with varying outlooks on growth and valuation across different sectors.
social_text: Morgan Stanley names Nvidia a top pick, projecting an 18x CY27 EPS entry point. Barclays downgrades Blue Owl, while Piper Sandler upgrades CrowdStrike to overweight with a $520 price target amid a 21% YTD drop.

title: Top Wall Street analysts recommend these dividend stocks for enhanced returns
summary:
In the current volatile U.S. stock market, driven by fears of AI disruption and geopolitical tensions, investors are increasingly turning to dividend stocks as a means to enhance returns. Top Wall Street analysts have identified several dividend-paying companies that not only provide attractive yields but also demonstrate the ability to generate strong cash flows, ensuring the sustainability of their dividends. This strategic approach allows investors to mitigate risks associated with market fluctuations while still aiming for solid returns.

One standout recommendation is Williams Companies (WMB), a midstream energy firm that has recently raised its quarterly dividend by 5%, bringing it to 52.5 cents per share. With an annualized dividend of $2.10, WMB offers a yield of 2.84%. Analysts, including Jefferies' Julien Dumoulin-Smith, have expressed confidence in Williams' growth trajectory, raising their price targets and maintaining buy ratings. The company's strategic shift towards behind-the-meter power generation positions it favorably in the evolving energy landscape.

Analysts predict that Williams could achieve an impressive 12% to 13% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) in EBITDA through 2030, bolstered by long-term contracts in its Power Innovation business. This optimism reflects a broader trend among dividend stocks, where consistent cash flow generation and strategic growth initiatives are key factors in attracting investor interest, especially during uncertain market conditions.
social_text: Top analysts recommend dividend stocks for enhanced returns amid market volatility. Williams Companies (WMB) raised its dividend by 5%, now yielding 2.84%, with a bullish outlook for 12-13% EBITDA CAGR through 2030.

title: Investors beware: These stocks are the most at risk from AI disruption
summary:
Jefferies analysts have identified a significant number of companies that are at risk of disruption due to the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI). This comes at a time when the U.S. stock market is experiencing volatility, with fears that AI could fundamentally alter traditional business models across various sectors. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has seen a decline of over 23% this year, indicating a bear market trend that has affected major players like Robinhood and ServiceNow. Investors are particularly concerned about the potential for AI-driven sell-offs to extend beyond the tech sector, leading to broader market implications.

The analysis conducted by Jefferies involved creating an 'AI risk' basket that includes 150 stocks with market capitalizations exceeding $1 billion. These stocks were evaluated based on their vulnerability to AI-related disruptions, which could manifest as asset repricing, demand substitution, labor substitution, moat decay, and pricing pressure. The firm utilized an AI-assisted search algorithm to pinpoint these risks, combining insights from various sub-industries that may face challenges due to AI advancements.

Desh Peramunetilleke, Jefferies' head of quantitative strategy, noted that while there has been a recent rebound in the software sector, it remains susceptible to further developments in AI. Currently, the software sector is trading at a price-to-earnings (PE) ratio of 21, which is on par with the broader market, but uncertainties surrounding future growth could lead to a potential discount in valuations. This situation underscores the importance for investors to remain vigilant and informed about the evolving landscape shaped by AI technologies.
social_text: Jefferies identifies 150 stocks at risk from AI disruption, including major players like Robinhood and ServiceNow. The software sector is down 23% this year, trading at 21x PE amid ongoing market volatility.

title: Forget DeepSeek. China’s already released 5 new AI models and UBS prefers this one
summary:
In recent weeks, several Chinese tech companies have launched new generative AI models, overshadowing the anticipation surrounding the upcoming DeepSeek model. Notable releases include Alibaba's Qwen 3.5 and ByteDance's Seedance 2.0, but the spotlight has shifted to MiniMax, which debuted its M2.5 model in mid-February. This model has garnered attention for its competitive performance, rivaling Claude's Opus 4.6 while being offered at a significantly lower price point. UBS analysts have noted a surge in developers opting for MiniMax M2.5 over both DeepSeek's V3.2 and other U.S. counterparts, indicating a shift in preference within the AI community.

UBS's recent coverage of MiniMax includes a buy rating and a price target of 1000 Hong Kong dollars ($127.83), suggesting a potential upside of over 30% from its current trading price. The analysts highlighted MiniMax's rapid adoption, with its AI usage reportedly reaching one-third of Anthropic's Claude at just one-tenth of the cost. This pricing strategy, combined with a diverse range of AI tools that extend beyond text generation to include video and audio capabilities, positions MiniMax favorably in the competitive landscape.

In contrast, other Chinese AI startups like Zhipu and Moonshot are focusing on more niche applications, such as coding and task completion. As the anticipation for DeepSeek's new model builds, the market is witnessing a dynamic shift, with MiniMax emerging as a formidable player in the global enterprise AI market. The developments in this sector are indicative of a broader trend in AI innovation, as companies strive to capture market share and meet the growing demand for advanced AI solutions.
social_text: MiniMax's M2.5 model is gaining traction, achieving one-third of Anthropic's Claude usage at just one-tenth the price. UBS rates it a buy with a target of HKD1000, signaling over 30% upside potential.

title: Private equity stocks like Apollo are among the most oversold names in the S&P 500
summary:
The recent decline in major stock indexes has raised concerns among investors, particularly following the release of the producer price index data, which exceeded expectations. This unexpected inflationary signal has led to a cautious market environment, compounded by significant layoffs at tech company Block. The layoffs have intensified fears regarding the potential disruption caused by artificial intelligence across various sectors, including software and healthcare. As a result, many stocks, especially those in the private equity sector, have entered oversold territory, indicating a potential for a market rebound based on technical analysis.

Private equity firms, notably Apollo Global Management, have been highlighted as some of the most oversold stocks within the S&P 500. Apollo's relative strength index (RSI) plummeted to 24 after its shares dropped over 11% in just one week. Despite this decline, analysts maintain a buy rating on the stock, reflecting a belief in its long-term value. Other firms such as Blackstone, Ares Management, and KKR & Co. also find themselves in similar positions, grappling with declining returns and challenges in fundraising amid a tough economic backdrop.

The market's volatility has created a stark contrast between oversold and overbought stocks. While Apollo and its peers struggle with investor sentiment, companies like Dell Technologies have seen their stocks rise, with RSIs indicating overbought conditions. This divergence highlights the ongoing challenges in the private equity space, particularly as concerns about exposure to failing entities, such as the UK mortgage provider Market Financial Solutions, continue to loom over investors. As earnings season progresses, the market will be closely watching these trends to gauge potential recovery or further declines in the private equity sector.
social_text: Apollo Global Management's stock has dropped over 11% this week, with an RSI of 24, making it one of the most oversold in the S&P 500. Analysts still rate it a buy despite concerns over private credit exposure.

title: Morgan Stanley says stocks like Nvidia have more room to run in March
summary:
Morgan Stanley has identified several stocks that are well-positioned for growth as the market heads into March, highlighting Nvidia as a key player. The investment bank's analysts believe that despite current market uncertainties, Nvidia and other overweight-rated stocks such as Cummins, Grab Holdings, Nasdaq, and Citigroup have significant potential for upward movement. Analyst Divya Gangahar Kothiyal emphasized Grab Holdings as an 'AI winner,' citing its diverse business segments and positive growth catalysts that could enhance user engagement and expand its total addressable market in on-demand services. Although Grab's shares have declined by 15% this year, Morgan Stanley advises investors to consider this a buying opportunity.

In addition to Grab, Citigroup has been singled out as a strong investment choice. Analyst Manan Gosalia expressed optimism about the bank's performance, forecasting robust revenue growth and an acceleration in share buybacks. He noted that the upcoming investor day on May 7 will be crucial, as it is expected to reveal an increase in the bank's return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) target, with aspirations for mid-teens by 2030. Citigroup's shares have appreciated nearly 40% over the past year, making them an attractive option for investors.

Cummins, a leader in power generation, also received a positive outlook from Morgan Stanley following its recent earnings report. The firm raised Cummins' price target to $675 per share from $600, indicating strong confidence in the company's growth trajectory. Analysts believe that Cummins has ample room for further gains, reinforcing its status as a top pick. Overall, Morgan Stanley's insights suggest that these stocks are well-positioned to capitalize on upcoming market trends and investor sentiment, making them worthy of consideration for investment portfolios.
social_text: Morgan Stanley highlights Nvidia, Cummins, Grab Holdings, and Citigroup as top stocks for March. Grab is an AI winner with diverse segments; Citigroup targets ROTCE above 10-11% by 2030. Cummins' price target raised to $675.

title: I Asked ChatGPT Which Retiree Expenses Will Rise Most in 2026: Here’s What It Said
summary:
As retirees prepare for 2026, they must be aware of the rising costs associated with healthcare and long-term care. According to insights from ChatGPT, healthcare expenses, particularly Medicare premiums and out-of-pocket costs, are expected to increase significantly. The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) indicates that Medicare Part B premiums could rise by $18 to $26 monthly, which will directly impact retirees' budgets. Additionally, prescription drug costs may also see increases due to the uneven implementation of caps from the Inflation Reduction Act, particularly affecting those whose incomes exceed certain thresholds, leading to surcharges that can add up to $81.20 or more for individual filers with adjusted gross incomes between $109,000 and $137,000.

Moreover, retirees are likely to experience heightened healthcare utilization during these years, which means that any cost-of-living adjustments (COLAs) from Social Security may be quickly absorbed by rising healthcare expenses. This situation leaves retirees with less financial flexibility for other essential costs, emphasizing the need for careful financial planning. The rising costs of healthcare are compounded by the fact that many retirees may not have anticipated such significant increases, making it crucial for them to reassess their budgets and savings strategies.

In addition to healthcare, long-term care and in-home assistance costs are also projected to rise. Staffing shortages and limited availability in care facilities are driving up expenses for retirees who require these services. As demand for long-term care increases, so too will the costs associated with hiring home health aides or securing care in facilities. Retirees must be proactive in understanding these trends to ensure they are financially prepared for the challenges that lie ahead in 2026.
social_text: In 2026, retirees face rising healthcare costs, with Medicare premiums increasing by $18-$26/month. Long-term care expenses are also set to rise due to staffing shortages, impacting financial planning significantly.

title: March 2026 housing market forecast: Key factors impacting home buyers and sellers this month
summary:
The March 2026 housing market forecast indicates a gradual improvement in home affordability, although the changes may not be significant enough to alter the plans of many buyers and sellers. Jim Breeze, senior vice president of PNC Bank, anticipates that March will mirror last year's trends, with a slight improvement expected. Historically, the mortgage industry experiences a surge in applications from January to April, with March marking the beginning of this uptick. Breeze emphasizes the importance of planning ahead, advising potential buyers to consult with mortgage advisors early to explore various affordability options, including down payment assistance programs that could facilitate home purchases.

A recent analysis by Zillow highlights that home affordability has improved by over $30,000 compared to the previous year, primarily due to rising incomes and decreasing mortgage rates. This improvement means that a median-income household can now afford a home priced at approximately $331,483, the highest level of affordability since March 2022. The decline in mortgage rates, which have reached their lowest since September 2022, is a crucial factor contributing to this enhanced affordability, making it a more favorable time for prospective homebuyers.

As the housing market gears up for the busy summer season, the forecast suggests that while affordability is on the rise, it may still require strategic planning and awareness of available resources for buyers. The combination of improved financial conditions and the potential for increased mortgage applications indicates a cautious optimism in the market, encouraging both buyers and sellers to prepare for the upcoming months. With the right guidance and information, many may find their path to homeownership more attainable than before.
social_text: In March 2026, home affordability improved by over $30K, allowing median-income households to afford homes priced at $331,483. Mortgage rates have fallen to 2022 lows, enhancing buyer prospects.

title: Rosenblatt says Middle East conflict boosts Palantir’s government pipeline, gives it an edge over rivals
summary:

Analyst John McPeake raised his price target for Palantir from $150 to $200, indicating a potential gain of 38%. He believes that the ongoing conflict will showcase Palantir's unique capabilities, allowing it to leverage its technology more effectively than other large language models (LLMs). McPeake emphasized that the current geopolitical climate underscores the necessity for advanced analytics and decision-making tools in defense, positioning Palantir favorably in the market.

The analyst's insights come in the wake of Anthropic's announcement that its AI models should not be utilized in fully autonomous weapons, leading the Trump administration to mandate a six-month phase-out of Anthropic's technologies across U.S. agencies. This development is expected to create opportunities for Palantir, as government entities seek alternatives that can meet their operational needs. McPeake noted that the demand for comprehensive wartime solutions is likely to result in significant contracts, similar to the recent deal with the U.S. Army that consolidated 75 contracts into a single agreement with Palantir.

Despite a challenging year where Palantir's shares have dropped 18%, the company has experienced a remarkable 74% increase over the past 12 months. McPeake argues that Palantir's growth trajectory is impressive, especially considering that it has achieved this growth organically, unlike other tech giants that relied on acquisitions during similar growth phases. He believes that Palantir's unique value proposition in the current geopolitical landscape justifies a premium valuation, reinforcing the company's potential for future success in the defense sector.
social_text: Rosenblatt's John McPeake raises Palantir's price target to $200, citing Middle East tensions as a catalyst for growth in government AI applications, with a projected 38% gain. Palantir's organic growth outpaces rivals.

title: JPMorgan turns bullish on Netflix, says streamer is better insulated from AI disruption risk
summary:
JPMorgan has recently upgraded its outlook on Netflix, shifting its rating from neutral to buy, with a new price target of $120, indicating a potential 25% increase. This change comes after the streaming service opted out of acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery, favoring a superior offer from Paramount Skydance. Analyst Doug Anmuth highlighted Netflix's robust fundamentals, emphasizing the company's commitment to margin expansion and its ability to generate strong free cash flow. He noted that the termination fee of $2.8 billion from the Warner deal could facilitate elevated share repurchases in 2026, further enhancing shareholder value.

Anmuth's analysis suggests that artificial intelligence will serve as a tailwind for Netflix rather than a disruption. He believes that AI will enhance content discovery, improve advertising solutions, and ultimately lower production costs. While competitors may leverage AI for content creation, Anmuth argues that Netflix's focus on storytelling and talent will provide a competitive advantage, insulating it from potential risks associated with AI-driven models.

Looking ahead, Anmuth anticipates continued growth in Netflix's viewing hours, particularly for original content, which saw a 9% increase in the latter half of 2025. He also hinted at a possible price increase in the U.S. market later this year, alongside expectations that Netflix's ad revenue could double, further solidifying its financial position. Overall, JPMorgan's bullish stance reflects confidence in Netflix's strategic direction and its ability to navigate the evolving landscape of streaming and content production.

Ritchie believes these factors will enhance Forgent's valuation and competitive edge in the market over the coming years.

title: All the highlights from Berkshire CEO Abel's first shareholder letter
summary:
In his inaugural letter to Berkshire Hathaway shareholders, CEO Greg Abel reaffirmed the company's longstanding culture and values, emphasizing continuity rather than change. He acknowledged the significant legacy left by Warren Buffett, whom he referred to as 'arguably the greatest investor of all time.' Abel's approach reflects a commitment to preserving the core principles that have guided Berkshire Hathaway under Buffett's leadership, ensuring that the company's ethos remains intact for future generations. He highlighted the importance of maintaining the culture as a treasured asset, a sentiment echoed by Vice Chairman Charlie Munger during previous meetings.

Abel's letter also addressed shareholder expectations regarding buybacks and dividends, but he maintained a cautious stance similar to Buffett's. He reiterated that share repurchases would occur only when the stock trades below their intrinsic value, a strategy aimed at enhancing value for existing shareholders. This conservative approach underscores Berkshire's commitment to long-term value creation rather than short-term gains, aligning with the company's historical investment philosophy.

Overall, Abel's communication signals a steady hand at the helm of Berkshire Hathaway, reassuring investors that the company's foundational principles will remain unchanged. His leadership style, while distinct from Buffett's, aims to uphold the legacy of excellence and integrity that has defined Berkshire Hathaway for decades, ensuring that the company continues to thrive in the future.

title: Gold has been on a run all year. Here's how to avoid a tax hit.
summary:
Gold has experienced a remarkable surge in value, reaching an impressive $5,300 per ounce on March 2, 2026, following a tumultuous start to the year. This increase can be attributed to various geopolitical tensions, including the ongoing conflict between the U.S. and Iran, as well as the implications of a recent Supreme Court ruling affecting tariff powers. For investors who have held onto gold, the past five years have seen a staggering 200% increase in prices, with gains exceeding 830% since 2006, marking a significant financial opportunity for those who remained invested in this asset.

However, potential sellers should be cautious about the tax implications associated with cashing in on their gold investments. Unlike stocks, the IRS categorizes gold as a collectible, which subjects it to a different tax structure. While short-term capital gains on gold are taxed similarly to stocks, selling gold after holding it for more than a year triggers the collectible tax rate, which can be as high as 28%. This distinction is crucial for investors to understand in order to avoid unexpected tax liabilities.

In summary, while the current gold rally presents lucrative opportunities for investors, it is essential to navigate the tax landscape carefully. Understanding the IRS's treatment of gold as a capital asset and the implications of holding periods can help investors maximize their returns while minimizing tax burdens. As the market continues to evolve, staying informed about both market trends and tax regulations will be key for gold investors looking to capitalize on this asset's potential.
social_text: Gold prices surged to $5,300/oz on March 2, 2026, driven by geopolitical tensions. With a 200% increase over 5 years, investors must navigate the IRS's collectible tax structure for potential gains over 1 year, capped at 28%.

title: AutoZone's second-quarter profit falls after inflationary headwinds
summary:
AutoZone has reported a decline in its second-quarter profit, primarily due to inflationary pressures that have negatively impacted the company's margins. The auto parts retailer, based in Memphis, Tennessee, experienced a drop in net income to $469 million, or $27.63 per share, down from $488 million, or $28.29 per share, in the same quarter last year. This decline comes despite an overall increase in sales, which rose by 8.15% to approximately $4.27 billion, although this figure fell short of analysts' expectations of $4.31 billion. The company's shares fell about 6% in premarket trading following the disappointing results, reflecting investor concerns over the impact of external factors on profitability.

The company faced several challenges over the past year, including tariffs, winter storms, and fluctuations in the vehicle market. Despite these hurdles, AutoZone's domestic segment saw a boost from both Do-It-Yourself and commercial sales during the quarter, indicating a steady consumer demand for auto parts. However, the winter storms in January caused disruptions that likely contributed to the overall profit decline. The mixed results highlight the ongoing struggle for retailers in the current economic climate, where inflation continues to exert pressure on margins.

Looking ahead, AutoZone will need to navigate these inflationary headwinds while capitalizing on the steady demand for auto parts. The company's ability to manage costs and adapt to market conditions will be critical in maintaining profitability and investor confidence. As the auto parts market evolves, AutoZone's strategic responses to these challenges will be closely monitored by analysts and investors alike, as they seek to understand the company's future performance in a competitive landscape.
social_text: AutoZone's Q2 profit fell to $469M ($27.63/share) amid inflationary pressures, despite an 8.15% sales increase to $4.27B. Analysts expected $4.31B in sales, causing a 6% drop in shares.

title: If you’re feeling behind financially, money dysmorphia may be distorting how you stack up. How to reset your perspective
summary:
The concept of money dysmorphia highlights a growing concern among individuals who, despite achieving financial milestones such as earning six figures, owning a home, and contributing to retirement, still feel inadequate about their financial status. This phenomenon is not a medical diagnosis but rather a psychological perception where one's financial health is misaligned with reality. The proliferation of social media and viral content showcasing wealth can exacerbate these feelings, leading individuals to compare themselves against unrealistic benchmarks that do not reflect the true financial landscape.

A significant factor contributing to money dysmorphia is the reliance on average net worth figures, which can be misleading due to the influence of ultra-wealthy households. Averages can skew perceptions, making typical households feel as though they are falling behind. In contrast, median net worth figures provide a more accurate representation of financial health across different age groups. For instance, the median net worth for U.S. households under 35 is approximately $40,000, while those aged 65-74 have a median net worth of around $410,000. These figures illustrate that if individuals are near these medians, they are not failing financially but rather are in line with the typical American household.

Understanding the difference between average and median net worth is crucial for resetting one's financial perspective. The article emphasizes that while social media can create a distorted view of wealth, recognizing where one stands in relation to median figures can help alleviate feelings of inadequacy. By focusing on realistic benchmarks, individuals can foster a healthier relationship with their finances and combat the negative effects of money dysmorphia, ultimately leading to a more balanced and confident financial outlook.
social_text: Money dysmorphia can distort financial perceptions, making six-figure earners feel inadequate. Median U.S. household net worth for ages 35-44 is ~$135K, showing many are financially typical despite social media distortions.

title: The candidate that Silicon Valley built is now the one they want to tear down
summary:
Ethan Agarwal, a tech entrepreneur with no prior political experience, has officially announced his candidacy for California's 17th congressional district, aiming to challenge incumbent Representative Ro Khanna. This race is expected to attract significant financial backing, potentially making it one of the most expensive primary challenges in the 2026 election cycle. Agarwal's entry into the political arena highlights the growing influence of Silicon Valley's billionaire class in shaping political outcomes, particularly against candidates like Khanna, who advocate for progressive tax policies.

Ro Khanna, a prominent Democrat and potential 2028 presidential candidate, has garnered attention for his support of a wealth tax in California, which has drawn the ire of some of the state's wealthiest tech founders and investors. His proposed legislation, co-sponsored with Senator Bernie Sanders, aims to impose a 5% annual wealth tax on individuals with a net worth of $1 billion or more, with projections estimating a revenue increase of $4.4 trillion over the next decade. This bold move positions Khanna as a significant figure in the ongoing debate over wealth inequality in America.

The dynamics of this race are particularly intriguing given Khanna's own history as a tech-supported outsider when he first ran for office in 2014. Back then, he was backed by influential figures such as Marc Andreessen and Sheryl Sandberg, but now he faces a well-funded challenger in Agarwal, who has connections to major venture capital firms. The irony of the situation is palpable, as Agarwal steps into a role that mirrors Khanna's past, raising questions about the influence of money in politics and the evolving landscape of Silicon Valley's political affiliations.
social_text: Ethan Agarwal, a tech entrepreneur, is challenging Rep. Ro Khanna in California's 17th district. Khanna's wealth tax proposal could raise $4.4 trillion, igniting a high-stakes primary funded by Silicon Valley elites.

title: Earnings live: Best Buy stock jumps despite softer holiday demand, Target stock rises
summary:
Best Buy's recent earnings report revealed a surprising decline in same-store sales during the crucial holiday shopping season, with a 0.8% drop in the fourth quarter. This downturn was unexpected, as analysts had anticipated a modest 0.2% increase following two consecutive quarters of positive growth. CEO Corie Barry noted that while the company's overall market share remained stable, there was a noticeable dip in customer demand across the industry during this period. Despite the sales slump, Best Buy's adjusted earnings per share exceeded expectations, coming in at $2.61 compared to the anticipated $2.46.

The company's revenue for the fourth quarter reached $13.81 billion, slightly below the $13.88 billion forecasted by Wall Street. For the full fiscal year, Best Buy reported total revenue of $41.69 billion, just shy of the $41.76 billion projection. The adjusted earnings per share for the year were $6.43, surpassing the expected $6.31. However, the overall same-store sales growth for the year was only 0.5%, falling short of the 0.9% increase that analysts had predicted.

Looking ahead, Best Buy is cautiously optimistic about the first quarter of 2027, projecting a return to growth with same-store sales expected to rise by 1%. The company anticipates revenue for the year to fall between $41.2 billion and $42.1 billion, with adjusted earnings per share forecasted in the range of $6.30 to $6.60. This outlook reflects a challenging retail environment, but Best Buy remains focused on regaining momentum in a competitive market.
social_text: Best Buy reported a 0.8% decline in same-store sales for Q4, with revenue at $13.81B, below expectations. Adjusted EPS was $2.61, exceeding forecasts. For 2027, revenue is projected between $41.2B-$42.1B.

title: Leadership change at All4Labels as CEO retires
summary:

The advisory board, led by chairman Harald Schulz, has expressed confidence in Hartinger's leadership and looks forward to her contributions as the company continues to innovate in the digital printing and sustainable packaging sectors.
social_text: All4Labels announces leadership change as Guenther Weymans retires, with Evelyn Hartinger stepping in as CEO. Hartinger aims to expand market focus and drive sustainable growth, building on last year's successful restructuring.

title: Google searches for ‘can’t sell house’ at all-time high — expert warns of housing crash ‘worse than 2008.’ Do this now
summary:
Recent data indicates that Google searches for 'can’t sell house' have reached an all-time high, signaling a potential crisis in the U.S. housing market. Housing analyst Melody Wright warns that the current disconnect between home prices and household income could lead to a correction worse than the 2008 financial meltdown. According to a report from Realtor.com, the typical U.S. household earns approximately 46% less than what is required to afford a median-priced home, highlighting the growing affordability gap. This situation is exacerbated by a significant decline in homebuyers, with January 2026 seeing an 8% year-over-year drop to just 1.36 million buyers, the lowest level recorded. 

The imbalance in the housing market is stark, with a recent Redfin report revealing that there are around 600,000 more home sellers than buyers, creating a 44% gap. While this typically suggests a buyer's market, the reality is that many potential buyers are unable to afford homes due to high prices and economic uncertainty. This has led to a retreat among house hunters, further complicating the market dynamics. 

Wright's prediction of a correction that aligns median household income with median home prices raises concerns about the potential for widespread foreclosures and a significant loss of household wealth. The 2008 housing crisis resulted in trillions of dollars in losses, and experts fear that the current trends could lead to an even more severe downturn. As affordability continues to be a critical issue, stakeholders in the housing market must pay close attention to these emerging trends to navigate the potential fallout effectively.
social_text: Google searches for 'can’t sell house' hit an all-time high as affordability gaps widen; 46% less income than needed for median homes. Experts warn of a housing correction worse than 2008, with 600k more sellers than buyers.
